channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Hostages freed after German bank robbery, suspects on run
item.description: Two hostages have been freed unhurt after being held in a bank in the German town of Sinzig, but despite heavily-armed police surrounding the bank, the suspects were able to escape.

media:group.media:description: Two captives were locked inside the bank's vault but were freed by police unharmed.

item.title: Migration, crypto and red tape to feature in budget bid for economic growth
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says Australia's productivity problem will take a long time to fix, but a $10 billion annual saving on business compliance costs is a start.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers says the government needs to act "on a broad range of fronts" to fix Australia's productivity problem.

item.title: Japan confirms first fatal bear attack this year with two more suspected
item.description: Japanese authorities say a 55-year-old woman found dead last month was killed by a bear. It is the first confirmed fatal bear attack for 2026 following a record 13 deaths last year.

media:group.media:description: A boom in bear numbers in Japan has led to increasing encounters.

item.title: Probe makes adverse findings against former ANU Council members
item.description: An independent review into 36 allegations concerning the Australian National University has made a series of adverse findings against former council members.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: An independent review into 36 allegations concerning the ANU has found a series of adverse findings.

item.title: 'I don't coach for my job': Voss defiant after latest loss, Dogs edge Port
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss is frustrated after a close loss to Brisbane, but denies he is coaching for his job, while the Western Bulldogs hold off Port Adelaide despite being held to one goal in the second half.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva and Andrew McGarry

media:group.media:description: Michael Voss has said his team cannot become comfortable with mediocrity after a close loss to Brisbane.

item.title: Government backbencher criticises 'giant, bloated, ineffective' NT public service
item.description: A Northern Territory government backbencher has criticised the "giant, bloated and ineffective" NT public service, saying its current size is not feasible.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: Tanzil Rahman says the NT public service is too large.

item.title: SA premier 'very concerned' over Whyalla steelworks' blast furnace
item.description: Whyalla steelworkers are struggling to get the city's blast furnace producing iron again, with SA Premier Peter Malinauskas saying he is "very concerned" about the situation.
item.dc:creator: James Wakelin

media:group.media:description: The Whyalla steelworks went into administration more than a year ago.

item.title: Man who drunkenly performed Nazi salute in front of cops fined $1,400
item.description: The man pleaded guilty to four charges, with a magistrate describing the Nazi gesture charge as a very serious offence.

media:group.media:description: The incident happened outside the Observatory Bar on Hobart's waterfront.

item.title: 'We don't want the penalty': Moses wins the game but doesn't end it
item.description: Mitchell Moses stuns the Cowboys in a bizarre golden-point finish, after Parramatta crosses twice in the final six minutes of normal time.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Mitch Moses's game-winning field goal strangely didn't end the game.

item.title: Dozens charged over seizure of largest child porn stash in NSW history
item.description: A probe into a cloud-based image-sharing folder resulted in the largest amount of child abuse material files seized in a single investigation in NSW history.
item.dc:creator: Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: Matthew Roberts was arrested as part of a statewide investigation into an alleged child abuse material sharing network.

item.title: Russia to make major international sporting return in Australia
item.description: Russia will be given its highest profile platform since its sporting exile at the World Aquatics Water Polo World Cup finals in Sydney in July this year.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: The Russian flag is returning to world sporting events.

item.title: NT taxpayers to be hit with $75m bill if fracking project fails
item.description: Northern Territory taxpayers will be hit with a $75 million bill if Tamboran Resources, one of the key Beetaloo Basin fracking companies, is unable to repay its lenders.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: The Northern Territory could be liable to pay $75 million if Tamboran Resources, a key player in the Beetaloo Basin, defaults on its fracking project.

item.title: Driver was on bail and probation when his 'drag racing' car hit and killed a cyclist
item.description: Lachlan Robert Carver has been sentenced to 10 years for a hit-and-run crash that killed cyclist Matthew Nilsen.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: Lachlan Carver (pictured) will spend eight years in jail for hitting and killing Matthew Nilsen.

item.title: Students receive ransom messages after learning system cyberattack
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of students have been unable to access schoolwork or submit assessments as access to Canvas has been paused.
item.dc:creator: Ned Hammond and Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: A ransom message received by students and teachers.

item.title: Man 'assisting detectives' with investigation into Adelaide woman's death
item.description: Police say a man is speaking to them in relation to the death of a woman whose body was found outside a block of units overlooking Henley Beach on Thursday.

media:group.media:description: Police on the Esplanade at Henley Beach where the woman's body was found.

item.title: Chinese oil tanker hit in reportedly first attack since Hormuz crisis
item.description: The chief engineer of a Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near the Strait of Hormuz says it is "still unclear" who fired on the vessel.
item.dc:creator: Xiaoning Mo

media:group.media:description: The JV Innovation attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz hours after joint US-Israeli military strikes on Iran but failed.

item.title: Plaintiff's lawyer describes Rebel Wilson as 'fantastical liar' in court
item.description: Final arguments in the defamation trial between Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson have heard claims of a suspicious career jump to "terrible, terrible" allegations.
item.dc:creator: Millie Roberts and Anton Rose

media:group.media:description: Actor and director Rebel Wilson (second from left) is being sued for defamation by actor Charlotte MacInnes (centre).

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's father won't be released from prison for sorry business
item.description: The father of Kumanjayi Little Baby will remain in prison for aggravated assault, despite calls from his lawyers for his release to attend cultural grieving for his daughter.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: The court ordered Raphael Granites remain in custody while cultural grieving for his daughter takes place.

item.title: Inmate’s multiple requests for medical support unanswered, inquest hears
item.description: A Queensland prisoner died in prison of a rare bacterial condition after his calls for medical help went unanswered for weeks, a medical expert has told a coronial inquest.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Nunes-Malek and James C Taylor

media:group.media:description: The Maryborough Correctional Centre is about 100 inmates over capacity.

item.title: Why there's no end in sight for Kezie Apps, Origin's ageless wonder
item.description: Kezie Apps will be the oldest State of Origin player across both states and the men's and women's series this year, but the 35-year-old Blues stalwart shows no signs of slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Kezie Apps will line-up for her 17th Origin match next Thursday night.

item.title: Bank hung up on pope when he tried to update his details, Chicago priest says
item.description: Father Tom McCarthy says Leo had trouble convincing his bank of his identity after being elected to lead the Catholic Church.

media:group.media:description: Leo was elected to lead the Catholic Church a year ago.

item.title: Three hikers killed in volcano eruption in Indonesia with 17 others rescued
item.description: Mount Dukono erupted on Friday morning, local time, sending plumes of ash some 10 kilometres into the sky.

media:group.media:description: Mount Dukono is one of Indonesia's most active volcanoes.

item.title: Bega's costs are up 10pc and customers will pay 'a large chunk' of it
item.description: One of Australia's biggest dairy manufacturers, Bega, is warning about soaring food inflation, as the war in the Middle East disrupts global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Emilia Terzon

media:group.media:description: Bega warns it will need to pass on higher costs driven by the Iran war to shoppers.

item.title: Anika Wells repays $10,000 in travel costs that broke rules
item.description: An audit of Communications and Sports Minister Anika Wells's travel by the Independent Parliamentary Expenses Authority found four breaches, but cleared her controversial trips to New York and Thredbo.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong and Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Sports and Communications Minister Anika Wells has repaid more than $10,000 to the taxpayer.

item.title: Melbourne men face court over allegedly sharing ISIS propaganda
item.description: The AFP alleges the four men had been "actively engaging" in sharing ISIS-inspired material, with the arrests following an investigation that began at Melbourne Airport.

media:group.media:description: The AFP's investigations began in 2024 following a luggage check at Melbourne Airport.
